retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Partial pulpotomy
retrieved_docs.content: Partial pulpotomy involves amputating the pulp 2 mm below the exposure site, where the tissue is presumed to be healthy. [16] This technique is the treatment of choice for young teeth with open apices when pulp capping is contraindicated, such as when excessive time has elapsed since the trauma or when the exposure diameter is too large. Partial pulpotomy serves as a provisional yet long-term approach, allowing the tooth to remain vital for as long as possible. However, root canal treatment followed by appropriate restoration may eventually be required. Restorative options include freehand or indirect composite resin restoration, fragment reattachment if available, and ceramic restoration.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The ConsAC is best suited for vital or minimally compromised anterior teeth, where preserving pericervical dentin is a priority. ConsAC is typically chosen for teeth with straightforward canal anatomy and minimal calcification, particularly when long-term fracture resistance is a central treatment goal.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Direct Pulp Capping
retrieved_docs.content: Direct pulp capping with Biodentine has been explored in various clinical scenarios. Direct pulp capping protects exposed pulp and promotes pulp vitality by forming tertiary dentin. [1] The prognosis of direct pulp capping depends on the type of pulp exposure, with traumatic or iatrogenic exposures having better outcomes than carious exposures. [1] Recent bioactive materials, such as Biodentine, have improved the predictability of the procedure following carious exposures.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The TrussAC is recommended when maintaining structural integrity is paramount, yet entry into multiple canals is required. This approach is most applicable in situations where canal orientations allow separate, small entry points and where restorative longevity and functional durability are key considerations.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Direct Pulp Capping
retrieved_docs.content: It's important to note that the choice of direct pulp capping material should consider various factors, including the type, size, and site of the pulp exposure and patient age. Although Biodentine appears promising, more research, especially with larger sample sizes, is needed to establish its clinical relevance and success rates compared to other materials like MTA and CH.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The TradAC is indicated in cases where extensive pulp necrosis or infection necessitates complete debridement of the pulp space. This method is also appropriate for teeth with severe pulp chamber calcification, where full visualization is crucial, and for retreatment cases that require a thorough examination of the canal system.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: Patients with severe caries with signs and symptoms of irreversible pulpitis or dental sepsis Crown was severely destroyed due to caries and is non-restorable Children at risk of bacterial endocarditis

retrieved_docs.title: Internal Tooth Whitening -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Internal tooth whitening is indicated for managing nonvital teeth with internal discoloration following endodontic treatment. Common causes of such discoloration include pulp chamber hemorrhage, pulp necrosis, remnants of pulp tissue, and residual endodontic materials. [5] In these cases, byproducts with chromogenic capacity penetrate the dentinal tubules, leading to discoloration of the surrounding dentin.

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Emergencies -- Treatment / Management -- Tooth Fractures
retrieved_docs.content: Pulp capping works best in immature teeth with open apices, where the pulp was considered healthy before the trauma. Indications for pulp capping include pulp exposure of a small diameter (less than 1.5 mm) that has only been exposed for a short time, thus minimizing the risk of bacterial invasion. [48]

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Direct Pulp Capping
retrieved_docs.content: For pulp exposures in carie-free permanent teeth, Biodentine and MTA have shown similar clinical results, with both materials promoting the formation of dentinal bridges and minimal inflammatory pulp responses. [1] In young permanent teeth with immature roots and carious exposures, Biodentine has demonstrated potential as a suitable capping material, showing high success rates in maintaining pulp vitality.

retrieved_docs.title: Tooth Fracture -- Treatment / Management -- Pulp capping
retrieved_docs.content: Also known as direct pulp protection, pulp capping involves applying a material with regenerative properties, commonly calcium hydroxide, to small pulp exposures. This technique is indicated when the pulp has been exposed for a short duration, as prolonged exposure increases the risk of bacterial invasion and irreversible inflammation. [14] Clinically, the exposure diameter should not exceed 1.5 mm. The likelihood of success is greater in teeth with healthy pulp before the trauma, immature teeth with open apices, younger teeth, and fractures without concomitant luxation injuries. [15]

retrieved_docs.title: Internal Tooth Whitening -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: Incomplete preparation of the access cavity can lead to the retention of pulp remnants within the pulp chamber, especially in the pulp horns, causing discoloration at the coronal level. [7] Similarly, inadequate removal of root-filling materials from the pulp chamber over teeth can cause tooth discoloration. This inadequate removal typically occurs when the root-filling materials remain elevated above the level of the bone. [5] In such cases, the prognosis of internal tooth bleaching depends on the type and duration of contact with the material. For example, stains from metallic compounds are more challenging to remove. [5] The remaining materials must be entirely removed with burs before bleaching can proceed. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontic Materials Used To Fill Root Canals -- Contraindications
retrieved_docs.content: There are few true contraindications for sealers and core obturation materials. One major contraindication is an allergy to any component of the material. [35] Allergies to constituent materials are rare but possible in every sealer and core material discussed, and the operator should take care to review all of a patient's possible allergies and be able to respond should a medical emergency arise. Other non-absolute contraindications are case-specific and depend on the treatment goal of the operator.

retrieved_docs.title: Endodontics, Pulp Space Anatomy and Access Cavity of Anterior Teeth -- Indications
retrieved_docs.content: The guided access technique is especially valuable for teeth with calcified pulp chambers or canals that are challenging to locate. This technique is also useful for retreatment cases where precision is needed to avoid unnecessary dentin removal and in anatomically complex situations that benefit from a preplanned, template-guided pathway. The guided access method is ideal when a minimally invasive approach is required despite challenging internal anatomy.

retrieved_docs.title: Overview of Periodontal Surgical Procedures -- Indications -- Clinical Crown Lengthening
retrieved_docs.content: Primary indication: subgingival caries, coronal fracture, or restorative margin that cannot be restored with standard restorative procedures for tooth isolation and restoration. The tooth must be otherwise restorable. Teeth undergoing crown lengthening and adjacent teeth must meet the following requirements:

retrieved_docs.title: Dental Materials: Biodentine, a Calcium Silicate Bioactive -- Clinical Significance -- Clinical Applications of Biodentine: Primary Teeth Pulpotomy
retrieved_docs.content: The study results show high success rates for Biodentine and formocresol pulpotomy techniques over 12 months, which can be attributed to proper protocol, isolation, aseptic conditions, and material handling. [5] The success rates were consistent with earlier research, emphasizing that Biodentine can be a suitable alternative for formocresol in primary teeth pulpotomy. [5] Furthermore, the simultaneous use of Biodentine as a dressing and restorative material offers a clinical advantage over formocresol, which requires additional restorative material in the pulp chamber. [5]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 2071
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures comprise a range of interventions used to manage complex obstetric conditions and facilitate safe delivery. Among these, the surgical treatment of ectopic pregnancy and abdominal pregnancy represents critical operative approaches for addressing abnormal implantation of the embryo, thereby preventing life‑threatening complications for the mother. In addition, Vaginal Assisted Delivery is included as a procedural option that supports the progression of labor and assists in the birth of the infant when additional support is required. Together, these child procedures under the umbrella of Pregnancy-Related Procedures illustrate the spectrum of surgical and assisted techniques employed in modern obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium gluconate is contraindicated in patients with قصور الكلى (renal failure), meaning its use should be avoided in individuals suffering from kidney failure.

retrieved_docs.content: is contraindicated in pancreatitis, and that pancreatic disorders unrelated to diabetes are mapped to pancreatitis. Pancreatitis is associated with increased systemic and tissue alkalinity, a factor that may affect diagnostic interpretation and therapeutic choices.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 2 -- Community 1174

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2347
retrieved_docs.content: In obstetric practice, the overarching topic “Indications for care in pregnancy; labor; and delivery” includes the specific condition “Malposition; malpresentation,” which is classified as a subcategory (has parent) within these care indications. This relationship highlights that malposition and malpresentation are considered part of the broader set of indications guiding clinical management during pregnancy, labor, and delivery.

retrieved_docs.content: or tissue transplantation). The unified taxonomy clarifies procedural indications, inter‑procedure connections, and associated pathologies, thereby supporting optimal patient assessment and surgical planning.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 1 -- Community 161
retrieved_docs.content: scarring into a unified classification scheme that supports recognition, documentation, and management of pregnancy‑related complications across multiple languages and clinical decision‑making pathways.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1799
retrieved_docs.content: Elective termination of pregnancy (إنهاء الحمل - اختياري) and forceps delivery (الولادة بالملقط) are clinically associated obstetric interventions. Both procedures are employed within the spectrum of pregnancy management: elective termination represents a planned ending of gestation, while forceps delivery is a method of assisted vaginal birth used when specific maternal or fetal conditions warrant intervention. Their clinical association highlights the range of obstetric care options available to address diverse maternal‑fetal indications, and recognizing this relationship assists clinicians in selecting the most appropriate intervention to optimize outcomes for both mother and newborn.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1981
retrieved_docs.content: Magnesium citrate is contraindicated in patients who have an intestinal perforation, as its use in the presence of this condition is not recommended.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2912
retrieved_docs.content: Pregnancy-Related Procedures is a broad category of obstetric interventions that encompasses specific techniques such as بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery, each classified as a child procedure within this group. Both بضع الفرج and Vaginal Assisted Delivery represent assisted vaginal delivery methods used to facilitate safe childbirth when spontaneous vaginal delivery is not optimal, highlighting the importance of tailored procedural options in maternal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 2 -- Community 997
retrieved_docs.content: positioning methods, and a spectrum of complications—including cervical dystocia, uterine atony, prolonged or stimulated labor, and pelvic‑malformation‑related difficult deliveries—providing a comprehensive framework for understanding labor‑related care and its associated indications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2791
retrieved_docs.content: Episiotomy and تمزق الأغشية الاصطناعي (artificial membrane rupture) are both classified under الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالتوليد (obstetric therapeutic procedures). By recognizing these interventions as subcomponents of the broader category of obstetric therapeutic care, clinicians can better organize treatment options, understand their related indications, and apply appropriate management strategies within the field of obstetrics.
